title: puccinellia porsildii
intro: Puccinellia porsildii, also known as Porsild's grass or saltwort grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the grass family Poaceae. It is native to the Arctic tundra regions of Canada and Greenland. Puccinellia porsildii has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be used in herbal preparations such as salves and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
